title: io.denna.defi.vault-config
description: Frontend-facing configuration for a single DeFi vault. Covers vault identity, share and deposit tokens, deposit/withdraw limits, attribution metadata, sub-markets or allocation buckets, fees, and compliance gating (terms of service, geo-blocking).

$schema.description: URI or relative path to this schema.

markets.description: Sub-markets or allocation buckets exposed by the vault (e.g., Morpho markets, Kamino reserves, Yearn strategies). Order is meaningful for display.

address.description: On-chain vault address or program account identifier.

chain.description: Chain the vault is deployed on (e.g., ethereum, base, solana). Mainnet vs. testnet is expressed by the consumer, not here.

protocol.description: Underlying vault protocol identifier (e.g., kamino, morpho, yearn, erc4626). Use lowercase. Helps consumers route to protocol-specific SDKs.

notes.description: Additional human-readable notes about the vault.

token.description: A token referenced by the vault — its on-chain address and display symbol. The same shape is used for the share token (vault receipt) and the deposit token (underlying asset).

decimals.description: Number of decimal places used on-chain. Present when the frontend cannot resolve it from chain metadata or wants to avoid an RPC round-trip.

limits.description: Deposit and withdrawal limits enforced by the frontend. On-chain limits may differ — this block captures the UX policy, not the protocol invariant.

minDeposit.description: Minimum deposit amount, denominated in the deposit token.

minWithdraw.description: Minimum withdrawal amount, denominated in the deposit token.

maxDeposit.description: Per-transaction maximum deposit. Omit for no frontend-side cap.

attribution.description: Attribution metadata — referral codes, memo programs, and other tagging used to trace deposits back to this frontend or a partner program.

referralCode.description: Referral or distribution code attached to deposits (e.g., embedded in an SPL memo or an EVM calldata tag).

memoProgram.description: Address of the memo program used to emit attribution (Solana: SPL Memo program). Omit for chains or protocols that use a different attribution mechanism.

notes.description: Human-readable notes about the attribution scheme.

market.description: A sub-market, reserve, or strategy the vault allocates into. For protocols that distinguish a parent lending market from per-asset reserves (e.g., Kamino, MarginFi), use `lendingMarket` for the parent account and `reserve` for the specific asset slot the vault deposits into. Single-account protocols can use `address` only.

id.description: Kebab-case identifier, stable across releases (e.g., main, prime, onre, maple).

description.description: Short description of the market's strategy or mandate.

collateralTypes.description: Collateral assets accepted by the market (e.g., ["SOL", "JitoSOL"], ["wBTC", "wETH"]).

address.description: Generic on-chain address of the market/reserve/strategy. Prefer the more specific `lendingMarket` and `reserve` fields when the protocol distinguishes them; `address` is retained for protocols that have a single canonical account or for backward compatibility.

lendingMarket.description: Parent lending-market account, when the protocol separates the market from its per-asset reserves (e.g., Kamino lending market, MarginFi group).

reserve.description: Per-asset reserve account inside the lending market — the on-chain account the vault actually deposits into (e.g., a Kamino USDS reserve). Consumers should prefer `reserve` over `address` when looking up an allocation by on-chain identifier.

fees.description: Vault-level fees visible to depositors. Protocol-level fees and per-market fees are out of scope for this block.

current.description: Currently active fee (e.g., management or performance fee). Use a denna rate with unit bps or percent.

performance.description: Performance fee, if distinct from the current/management fee.

notes.description: Human-readable notes about fee schedule, activation dates, or governance conditions.

compliance.description: Compliance gating applied by the frontend before allowing vault interaction.

enabled.description: Whether the Terms of Service gate is active. If false, the consumer skips ToS enforcement regardless of the other fields.

version.description: Current ToS version string. Consumers store acceptance keyed by this version so a bump re-prompts users.

required.description: Whether ToS acceptance is mandatory before any vault action. When true, `url` becomes required so users have somewhere to read what they are accepting.

url.description: URL where the current ToS document can be read. Required when `required` is true.

geoBlocking.description: IP-based geographic access policy. Implemented by the frontend edge (e.g., Vercel Middleware). When `enabled` is true, exactly one of `blockedCountries` or `allowedCountries` must be provided.

enabled.description: Whether geo-blocking is active. If false, the country lists are ignored.

blockedCountries.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes to block. Mutually exclusive with `allowedCountries`; the schema enforces exactly one when `enabled` is true.

allowedCountries.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes explicitly allowed. When present, all other countries are blocked. Mutually exclusive with `blockedCountries`.

notes.description: Human-readable notes — typically references to the legal basis or the review status of the list.
